title: linaria viscosa subsp. crassifolia
intro: Linaria viscosa subsp. crassifolia, commonly known as sticky toadflax or thick-leaved toadflax, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The leaves and flowers of Linaria viscosa subsp. crassifolia have been used in herbal preparations to aid in reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and promoting skin health. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues and providing antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
